mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rates crossing above 8 percent signal severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns exceed 55 percent of triggered instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate broad-based demand recovery, generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above its 12-month moving average confirms supply-side momentum that supports near-term equity price appreciation over the following week.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of observations during late-cycle expansions, or Rank IC drops below 0.005.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI increases crossing above 0.4 percent trigger expectations of monetary tightening, which discounts future cash flows and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of high-inflation trigger months, or predictive accuracy fails to exceed random chance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm labor market tightening that boosts wage growth and corporate revenues, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances, or interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2 percent reflects sustained economic expansion that supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of quarters meeting the growth threshold, or Rank IC remains below 0.015 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production indices crossing below 95 indicate severe manufacturing contraction that depresses earnings expectations and causes negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive returns exceed 50 percent when the index remains below the threshold, or signal loses predictive power during recovery phases (IC < 0.01).
